title: hedeoma pilosa
intro: Hedeoma pilosa, also known as false pennyroyal, is a perennial plant native to the United States, particularly in the eastern and central regions. It belongs to the mint family and is known for its small white or pink flowers and heart-shaped leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to repel insects and have antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems. Hedeoma pilosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of anxiety, stress, and other health issues, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine.
